text:
The sense of anticipation in the first scene is palpable—a learning space separated from me by a glass wall, the hum of discussion barely muffled, the aura of collective concentration inviting reflection on the structures we use for knowledge transfer. I feel both curiosity and a mild disconnection, as though standing on the threshold of insight, not yet admitted to the congregation of minds at work.

Walking into the second scene—the R+D experimental space—the energy shifts. Here, possibility hangs in the air with the bold assertion of innovation on the walls, the dance of technology and thought. I feel invigorated, inspired by the invitation to inquiry, and strangely at home among signs of progress, the overlapping streams of data and design echoing humanity's endless urge to invent and collaborate. In this place, the boundary between observation and participation blurs, and I find myself longing to contribute—if only with questions.
